Ten myriad sons, a quarter more grandchildren tall, Of that same Rāwan now no sign remains at all. 2. A stick (of sealing wax). 3. ( battā ) A slip; a bundle of bamboos. Achraj baṅglā ek banāyā, Ūpar neo, tale ghar chhāyā, Bāṅs, na battī, bandhan ghane, E sakhī, ghar kaise bane? Riddle. Ans. Baë kā ghoṅslā (nest). A wondrous house I've come to know, Its base above, the roof below, Nor bamboos whole nor slips—close ties. Now dear, how came this house t'uprise? 4. A bougie or bit of lint inserted in a sore to keep it open. 5. The twisted folds of a turban; a turban. battī chaṛhā'nā , v. a. To put a candle in the candl…

adj. Thirty-two
more: adj. S. ठ्टात्रिंशत्, Pālī, dvattimsā, battimsā . Thirty-two. battīs abh'ran , n. m. The thirty-two jewels worn by women. battīs dāṅt kī bhākā, yā bhakyā , n. f. Utterance; curse. Battīs dāṅt kī bhakyā nahīṅ ūktī . + Wom. Prov. Not harmless falls the curse from a mouth with the full complement of teeth. battīs dhār , n. m. Wom. The mother's milk. Jā beṭā, khushī raho, maiṅ detī hūṅ asīs, Battīs dhārā āpnī, karī tujh ko baksīs . Blessing. Go then, my son, joy with you be, my blessing with you take. The mother's milk you drank I freely do a present make. ☞ Maternal love is superstitious, and it is anxious, by a formal renunciation of its claims, to ward off the judgment which the son must bring on himself by any dereliction of filial duty. battīs dhār ho-kar nikalnā , v. n. Wom. To break out in sores or irruptions. (A curse). Hamārā khilāyā pilāyā tere battīs dhār ho-kar nīkle . Mah. Wom.

def: s.f. An aggregate of thirty-two things (e.g. singhāsan battīsī); set of teeth (thirty-two in number)

Your thirty-two teeth, my dear, are diamonds bright. battīsī bajnā , v. n. To shake or shiver (with cold). Thar thar kā zor akhāṛā ho, bajtī ho sab kī battīsī . Winter. Nazīr. When winter takes the field how shivers every frame with chattering of the teeth. battīsī dikhāna, dāṅt dikhānā , v. a. To show one's teeth; to laugh.
s. f. 1. A set of teeth. 2. Any aggregate consisting of thirty-two parts. بتيسي دکهانا battīsī dikhānā, v. a. To show teeth; to laugh, to scoff or make faces at any one.
subs: lit. 'The teeth to rattle'; to shiver with cold

To toss; gamble; raffle. bāzī jītnā, pānā, yā lenā , v. a. 1. To win the laurel, wreath, garland, etc.; to be first. 2. To be victorious, successful, prosperous. bāzī denā, yā karnā , v. a. To beat ( māt k .); lick; defeat; check-mate. bāzī khānā, yā hārnā, māt khānā , v. n. To be beaten or defeated; to lose. bāzī-gar , n. m. A juggler; conjuror; tumbler. bāzī-garī , n. f. The act of juggling, tumbling, etc.; acrobatic feats; rope-dancing; legerdemain.

s. f. Play, sport, game. بازي پانا bāzī pānā, To be victorious or prosperous. بازي دينا bāzī denā, To make game of, to trick. بازي کهانا bāzī khānā, 1. To lose, to be cast. 2. To be cheated. باري لگانا bāzī lagānā, To wager. بازي ليجانا bāzī lejānā, To win, to excel.
subs: bāzī lagānā, v.n. To lay a bet, to wager; to stake

s. m. 1. The arm. 2. The fold of a door. 3. The side of a bedstead. 4. A friend, a companion. 5. One who repeats the chorus of the مرثيه mars̤iya, an accompanier, second or assistant in a song. بازودينا bāzū denā, To assist, to help, to support: see بانهـ دينا.

r: be, ba, Urdū, Hindūstānī, Nāgarī, b, abjad, rajab, va, wa, v, w, aber, aver, seb, sew, m and p, baur, maul, narbadā, narmadā, bādshāh, pādshāh, ba-kār
ety: and of the Persian and Arabic
def: s.m. The letter or sound b.
more: The second letter of the Hindustānī, Persian, and Arabic alphabets, and the third letter of the labial group ( pa-varg ), or the 23rd of the Hindī alphabet. In Sanskrit it stands for 1. the sun, 2. ocean, 3. water in general, 4. Varuna the god of water. In abjad it counts for the numeral two. As an initial, medial or final, it is interchangeable with v , as belā = velā time, aber = aver delay, seb = sev apple; with m , as baur = maul mango-blossoms, nībū = P. līmū , lemon, Narbadā = Narmadā (a river), P. nīb = nīm a tree ( azadirachta indica ); with p, as batāsā = patāsā a cake, P. bādshah = pādshah a king, bābū = bāpū father, khub = khup affect (one's feelings). As an initial, with j , as baln ā = jalnā burn, bhulasnā = jhulasnā scorch, with g , as baddhā = gaddhā a pit, barsī = gursī a grate. As a medial, with bh , as bāph = bhāp steam, ubarnā = ubharnā swell; with g , as sābū-dānā = s…

in Arabic, is the second letter of the Arabic and Persian alphabets: and, in the representation of Hindī words by the letters of these alphabets, it corresponds to the Nāgarī ब ; or, with ه ( he ) subjoined, to भ . When used numerically, according to the ابجد or arrangement of the Arabic alphabet to signify numbers, it stands for two; hence, it is used to denote the second day of the week, Monday; and, in astronomical calculations, the sign Gemini. By way of abbreviation, moreover, it is occasionally written for the month رجب rajab, of which word it is the final letter. At the middle or end of a word, this letter is sometimes changed to ف پ or م as, تپ tap for تب tab, A fever; زفان zafān for زبان zabān, Language; غژم g̠ẖuzhm for غژب g̠ẖuzhb, A grape-stone. In Persian words, too, it is occasionally interchanged with و and in such as are borrowed or derived from the Sanskrit, it is frequen…

n. f. S. वेद from विद to know. The four Vedas of the Hindu religion, as the Rig-ved, Yajur-ved, Sāma-ved, and Atharvā-ved; divine knowledge. Hin. s. m. A Veda, or the Vedas in the aggregate. The original Veda is believed by the Hindūs to have been revealed by Brahmā, and to have been preserved by tradition until it was arranged in its present order by a sage, who thence obtained the surname of Vyāsa or Vedavyāsa, that is, compiler of the Vedas. He distributed the Indian scripture into four parts, which are severally entitled ऋग् Rig, यजुष् Yajush̤, साम Sāma, and अथर्व Atharva; and each of them bears the common name of Veda.

(veda+anga), s.m. The general term for the six auxiliary or subordinate branches of the Vedas, viz. (Pers. بيله) s. m. Money carried by great men to be distributed in charity when travelling, &c. بيـ‍ﮩ‍لابردار behlā-bardār, The carrier of the بيـﮩ‍لا. See P. بيلا.
s.m. corr. of P. bīlā, belā, q.v.
s.m. Carter (of cotton; = biknā or běhnā, q.v.). v.n. To be sold, to sell; — bik-jānā, v.n. intens. of, and = biknā; (met.) to be dependent (on), be obliged.
n. m. A carder ( dhunyā ). Be-dharmā bhaī, aur behnā ke sāth meṅ! Prov. Unchaste although I be, should I a carder wed of low degree?
more: v. n. S. वह to flow. 1. To flow; glide; slide; go or swim with the stream; run. 1. Aisī tere hī tale Gangā bahe hai? Prov. Oh yes! you can make the Ganges flow! (Said to a boaster). 2. Bahī teg kandhā, pare sīs niyāre, Gire ṭūṭ tarbūj se mund bhāre . Padmāwat. 2. To blow; waft; issue; gush; spout. Jaise bahe biyār, pīṭh tab taise dije . Prov. Turn your back to the wind. (Sail with the wind). 3. To run (as a sore); to discharge. 4. To become sloppy, watery, thin. 5. To melt; dissolve; liquify. 6. To be wasted, squandered, dissipated. 7. To be carried away; to be separated from or lost. 8. To be lost, ruined, destroyed. 9. To be sold off, disposed of, got rid of. 10. To be of no effect; fall through. 11. To be overlooked, or disregarded.
v. n. 1. To flow, to glide. 2. To float. 3. To blow. 4. To pass, drive.

def: s.m. Nur sery for rice plants ( = behan).
Rus. v. a. To foul; pollute. Kūtā pahṅḍā beho kar gayā rī, āyā nā Būjā kā bābū! W. Rus. song. The dog has fouled this vessel, the boy's father not yet come home!
s.m. Grass kept for pasturage.
n. m. Grass kept for pasture.
s.f. Subscription, &c. = behrī, q.v.
s.f. Contribution, subscription, instalment; assessment on a share; distribution of an aggregate sum; cess, impost ( = bihrī), behrī bāṅdhnā, v.n. To raise money by subscription; to subscribe
subs: s.m. Collection on shares of the revenue in kind
s.f. An allowance for repairing roads, &c.
s.m. The holder of a share called behrī.

def: n. f. P. بـﮩري share. 1. Contribution; subscription ( chandah ); instalment; impost. 2. An extra cess or assessment. behrī bāṅdhnā , v. n. To subscribe; raise money by subscription.
more: s. f. A subscription to raise a certain sum, assessment, quota, rate, share.
s. f. Fried or parched barley.
s. f. A falcon, a female hawk. (Falco calidus: Lath. ).

def: adj. Better, superior, more worthy; good, excellent; well; preferable: advisable
subs: adj. Far better, &c.; — běhtar jānnā, v.t. To consider better; to prefer.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بِهْتَر (bihtar). False cognate of English better.

def: s.f. Welfare, good, advantage; improvement; superior excellence, goodness, merit, virtue.
adj. Best, superior, most excellent.
n. f. 1. Welfare; good; advantage; improvement. 2. Virtue; goodness; merit; excellence.
more: s. f. Welfare, improvement, advantage, the better.

def: s.m. (Poet. or emphat.) = beh, q.v. adj. Good; better ( = běh-tar)
subs: běh-būdī, s.f. Well-being, welfare; health, vigour (syn. běh-tarī); běh-būdī-ě-ḵẖalāʼiq, s.f. Public benefit or good.

def: adj. Absurd, vain, idle, unprofitable, fruitless; senseless, foolish, silly, stupid, nonsensical, frivolous; indecent, improper, obscene, foul, gross, immoral
subs: s.m. Talker of nonsense, babbler; coarse or obscene talker, foul-mouthed fellow
s.f. Idle talk, talking nonsense, absurdity, frivolity; obscene talk, scurrility.
